title: Chesapeake Bay Seasoning
cScaleSummary: C1 because this seasoning blend contains no cholesterol-contributing ingredients. The salt, celery seed, paprika, bay leaf, thyme, dill, mustard, peppers, mace, nutmeg, ginger, red pepper flakes, and cayenne all contribute 0 mg cholesterol. The C1 rating is conservative for how the seasoning is intended to be used with seafood, but the seasoning blend itself contains 0 mg cholesterol..
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because this is a seasoning blend. It is low in cholesterol, but it is not structurally fiber-forward and does not meaningfully contribute legumes, whole grains, nuts, seeds, vegetables, or other LDL-supportive ingredients in serving-sized amounts.
intro: This style of seasoning grew out of early 20th-century coastal seafood houses, where bold spice blends were built to season large batches of crab and shrimp. This version leans New England, with a lighter hand on salt and a cleaner, more herbal profile that lets the shellfish stay front and center.

stepGroups.title: Instructions
items: Grind the celery seed ({{quantity:celery-seed}}) to a fine powder. This is critical. Whole or coarse celery seed won’t distribute evenly and you’ll lose that signature flavor.
Combine the ground celery seed and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt}}) in a bowl. Mix thoroughly so the celery flavor is evenly carried by the salt.
Add the sweet and smoked paprika ({{quantity:sweet-paprika}}, {{quantity:smoked-paprika}}). Whisk until the color is uniform.

Stir in the bay leaf ({{quantity:ground-bay-leaf}}), thyme ({{quantity:dried-thyme}}), dill ({{quantity:dried-dill}}), mustard ({{quantity:dry-mustard}}), and peppers ({{quantity:ground-black-pepper}}, {{quantity:ground-white-pepper}}). Mix well.

Add the mace ({{quantity:ground-mace}}), nutmeg ({{quantity:ground-nutmeg}}), ginger ({{quantity:ground-ginger}}), red pepper flakes ({{quantity:crushed-red-pepper-flakes}}), and cayenne ({{quantity:cayenne}}). Whisk until fully blended.
Pulse briefly in a spice grinder if needed for a tighter blend.
Let sit at least 12 hours before using.
